current_windows_app.ui_stack: PySide6 QGuiApplication + QQmlApplicationEngine loading ui/pyside_app/qml/MainWindow.qml with bundled ui/qml imports

components.name: CryptoHunter Windows EXE PySide6/QML shell
components.summary: PyInstaller spec builds CryptoHunter from ui/pyside_app/__main__.py; the app loads ui/pyside_app/qml/MainWindow.qml by default and bundles shared ui/qml imports.
components.roadmap_impact: Current EXE baseline is PySide6/QML; future UI decisions must preserve or intentionally replace this entrypoint.

components.name: ui/desktop Electron/React renderer
components.summary: React/Vite/Electron assets exist with TypeScript configuration, but the Windows EXE spec does not include ui/desktop and current packaging points at PySide6/QML.
components.roadmap_impact: Likely legacy or alternate frontend; audit unique screens before migration/removal decisions.

components.name: ui/qml shared QML component library
components.summary: Shared QML components are bundled by CryptoHunter.spec and imported by the PySide shell, but many panels are source/read-model surfaces rather than verified runtime execution controls.
components.roadmap_impact: Reusable visual foundation; distinguish runtime-backed panels from preview surfaces in later M0 elements.

components.name: LiveExecutionRouter and current primary adapter bootstrap
components.summary: LiveExecutionRouter can route across multiple adapters, but the GUI/frontend bootstrap currently wraps ExchangeManager as a single adapter registered under 'primary' with default_route ('primary',). Live build is also skipped when DUDZIAN_TEST_MODE is set.
components.roadmap_impact: Execution core has multi-adapter capability, while current GUI runtime path is effectively single ExchangeManager adapter named primary.

components.name: ExchangeManager native adapter registry
components.summary: ExchangeManager contains dynamic/native adapter registry loading from config/core.yaml with many exchange classes, paper simulators and sandbox enabling helpers; real runtime activation depends on config and environment.
components.roadmap_impact: Reusable exchange foundation, but M0 must define supported exchange/account topology and live gates.

components.name: Demo/paper/testnet/live environment representation and gates
components.summary: Code and docs contain demo, paper, testnet/sandbox and live concepts; live requires enabled runtime.execution.live, while UI preview and docs also expose demo/paper/live flows that are not equivalent to verified live trading.
components.roadmap_impact: M0 must normalize vocabulary and explicitly separate UI demo/preview, paper simulator, exchange sandbox/testnet and live.

components.name: Trading universe selection, AI candidates and market imports
components.summary: UI contains scanner/market/universe preview surfaces and fixtures/read models; no evidence was found that the current EXE executes autonomous live universe expansion from those screens.
components.roadmap_impact: Keep as product-contract input; do not treat preview candidates as implemented trading universe automation.

components.name: Strategies, scheduler and AI governor
components.summary: Strategy definitions, multi-strategy scheduler and AI governor modules/tests exist, but several UI AI Center flows are static read models or demo fixtures.
components.roadmap_impact: Reusable strategy/governor foundation; M0 must define binding of strategy instances to accounts, exchanges and pairs.

components.name: Risk, kill switch, secrets and licensing controls
components.summary: Risk/security modules include risk engines, kill-switch previews, secret management, licensing and signer/hardware-wallet tests; current UI has preview/read-only safety gates as well as backend controllers.
components.roadmap_impact: Strong reusable safety primitives, but M0 must classify hard runtime gates versus preview/status surfaces.

components.name: Persistence: SQLite, JSONL, Parquet, journals and updates
components.summary: Repository includes JSONL ledgers/journals, Parquet storage tests, SQLite/reporting/update package code and backup/update docs; coverage is fragmented across runtime, execution, reporting and updater modules.
components.roadmap_impact: M0 should establish canonical persistence boundaries for orders/fills, portfolio, backup and rollback.

components.name: Background runtime and operator shell behavior
components.summary: Offline daemon/local gateway/runtime service modules exist, but no verified evidence in the current EXE audit proves tray/autostart/HUD/reconnect semantics or background survival after window close.
components.roadmap_impact: High-risk M0 decision area; verify operator shell lifecycle before implementation.

components.name: Packaging, updater, artifact smoke and CI
components.summary: Windows CI installs desktop requirements, runs focused tests, builds PyInstaller onedir, verifies CryptoHunter.exe exists, verifies artifact contents, runs isolated --smoke-test and uploads ZIP/reports; local scripts mirror this build path.
components.roadmap_impact: Packaging is a usable foundation; updater/rollback contract still needs M0 boundary decisions.

environment_findings: ui/pyside_app preview modules model paper/testnet/live gates without proving runtime activation.
ExchangeManager can enable CCXT sandbox/testnet via setSandboxMode/set_sandbox_mode or test URLs.
roadmap_conflicts: Docs/UI preview flows mention live/demo/paper capabilities beyond what current EXE audit verifies as runtime execution.
legacy_or_duplicate_candidates: preview_* read models and static fixtures that duplicate runtime concepts
